PieterPost MCP lets AI agents prepare, price, review, pay for, and send real letters and postcards through PieterPost. It provides a safe postal outbox for MCP clients via a remote Streamable HTTP server at .

# PieterPost MCP

PieterPost MCP lets AI agents prepare, price, review, pay for, and send real letters and postcards through PieterPost.

## Server [...] ## Server

 MCP server URL: `
 Human docs: `
 Markdown docs: `
 OAuth issuer: `

## What It Does

PieterPost gives MCP clients a safe postal outbox. Agents can draft letters and postcards, quote prices, create review links, create hosted payment links, upload assets, use Mailbook contacts, and check order status.

For normal use, agents should create a PieterPost review or payment link. PieterPost sends the mail only after the user reviews and pays.

A standalone Windows MCP server that provides Claude with Google search and web research capabilities powered by SerpAPI. Requires a SerpAPI account and API key passed via the SERPAPI\_KEY environment variable. Supports stdio and HTTP transport modes. The HTTP service saves config in %USERPROFILE%\.google-research-mcp\config.toml when installed.

results.title: PieterPost MCP for ChatGPT and Claude
results.content:
LetterPostcardMailbook

PieterPost remote MCP server

# Send letters and postcards through chat.

Connect PieterPost to a compatible MCP client. Draft a message, choose a saved Mailbook contact, create a secure checkout link, and let PieterPost print, stamp, and mail it after you review the details.

## See the MCP flow in action

This walkthrough shows one compatible MCP client. The same PieterPost server URL and OAuth approval flow work across clients that support remote MCP.

## Connect PieterPost to your assistant

Paste this public remote MCP endpoint into ChatGPT, Claude, or another compatible client:

`

Popular AI workspaces use different names for connectors and MCP. Look for remote MCP or custom connector support; availability can vary by plan and product. [...] OpenAI MCP docs

1. 1Open ChatGPT settings and go to Apps & Connectors.
2. 2Create or add a custom connector, name it PieterPost, and paste the MCP server URL.
3. 3Choose OAuth when prompted, scan the available tools, then authorize your PieterPost account.
4. 4Start a chat with PieterPost enabled and ask for a letter, postcard, checkout link, or order update.

Claude logo

### Claude

Use PieterPost as a custom connector in Claude surfaces that support remote MCP servers and OAuth authorization.

Claude connector docs [...] Claude connector docs

1. 1Open Claude and go to Customize > Connectors.
2. 2Click the plus button, then choose Add custom connector.
3. 3Name it PieterPost and paste the MCP server URL.
4. 4Click Add, then Connect, and approve PieterPost when the browser opens.
5. 5Ask Claude to prepare a letter or postcard, then review the PieterPost link before paying.

MCP

### Other MCP clients

Use the same remote MCP endpoint from any client that supports streamable HTTP transport and OAuth-based authorization.

MCP authorization docs

# Agent mail MCP for AI Agents

Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Agent mail MCP or direct API to send emails, fetch inbox messages, organize threads, and automate email workflows through natural language.

Get started for freeGet a demo

Agent mail logoAgent mail

results.title: Why your AI agent needs its own inbox — Adi Singh, AgentMail | MCP Night
results.content:
# Why your AI agent needs its own inbox — Adi Singh, AgentMail | MCP Night
## WorkOS
6070 subscribers
9 likes

### Description
345 views
Posted: 27 May 2026
"I came across AgentMail and they require a human to first create an account... it's the biggest epic fail I've ever seen in my life." Adi Singh read that tweet, agreed with it, and spent the next week making sure an AI agent could sign up for AgentMail on its own — in a single prompt. This is the live demo from MCP Night.

0:00 AgentMail: the inbox API for AI agents
0:29 Read the tweet that broke us
1:17 Rebuild signup so agents can do it
1:51 Sign up an agent with one prompt (live)
3:19 Lock it down with allow-lists
4:36 Use email as the identity layer for agents [...] AgentMail:
